title: Overfitting vs. Underfitting Curves
concept: Loss curves are the window into your model's learning state. Comparing training loss and validation loss across epochs reveals whether your model is overfitting (memorizing) or underfitting (failing to learn).

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is a 'silent data bug' in deep learning?

options.text: A bug where the code runs fine without crashing, but the data is misaligned, causing poor performance
A compilation error in the convolutional layer
An error that only occurs when using silent GPUs

exercises.explain: Yes! Silent data bugs run without crashing but prevent effective learning.

cards.title: The Forking Paths
cards.body: As training progresses, your training loss drops lower and lower. But suddenly, your validation loss stops dropping and begins to climb back up. The curves are splitting apart. Your model has crossed the line from learning general features to memorizing the training set.

cards.title: Overfitting vs. Underfitting
cards.body:
- Underfitting: Both training and validation loss remain high and flat. The model is too simple or hasn't trained long enough.
- Overfitting: Training loss is very low, but validation loss is high or rising. The model is memorizing training details instead of generalizing.

cards.title: Studying the test questions
cards.body: Underfitting is like barely studying for a test: you fail both the practice quizzes and the real exam. Overfitting is like memorizing the exact answers to the practice quizzes: you score 100% on the practice set, but fail the real exam because the questions are worded slightly differently.

cards.title: Cat Memorization
cards.body: An overfit cat detector learns the exact background pixels of your 3 cat training photos. When shown a new cat in a forest, it fails because it's looking for the specific rug pattern from the training photos. Training loss is 0.01, but validation loss is 1.5.

cards.title: Training loss isn't the final score
cards.body: It's a myth that your training loss is the metric that matters most. A training loss of zero is often a bad sign, indicating extreme overfitting. The only curve that determines the quality of your model is the **validation loss** — it measures performance on unseen data.

cards.title: Underfitting vs. Overfitting Curves
cards.body: Comparing loss curves over training epochs:

left.text:
Train loss continues to drop
Val loss bottoms out and rises
Fix: Augmentation, regularization, dropout

right.text:
Both curves remain high and flat
Model is not learning enough
Fix: More parameters, longer training

exercises.prompt: What does a rising validation loss curve alongside a dropping training loss curve indicate?

options.text: Overfitting: the model is memorizing training data and failing to generalize
Underfitting: the model needs to be simplified
The learning rate is set too low
The model has achieved optimal generalization

exercises.explain: Correct! Diverging curves (dropping train, rising val) is the classic signature of overfitting.

exercises.prompt: True or false: An underfitting model is characterized by high loss on both the training set and the validation set.
exercises.explain: True. Underfitting means the model is failing to learn the patterns in the data, resulting in poor scores on both sets.

exercises.sentence: To fix overfitting, you should increase ___ (like augmentation or dropout) during training.
exercises.explain: Regularization restricts memorization, helping the model generalize to validation data.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is the most effective way to fix an underfitting CNN model?

options.text: Use a larger model architecture or train for more epochs
Apply aggressive data augmentation to make the task harder
Delete half of the training dataset to simplify the input

exercises.explain: Correct! Underfitting indicates lack of capacity or time, so a larger model or more epochs is the solution.

exercises.prompt: Your validation loss curve starts rising at epoch 10, while training loss drops to 0.05. You are already using data augmentation. What is the next logical step?

options.text: Use early stopping to halt training at epoch 10 before the model begins to overfit
Double the learning rate to force the model to correct its weights
Disable all augmentation to make training easier
Train for 100 more epochs to let the curves merge again

exercises.explain: Exactly! Early stopping stops training at the sweet spot of generalization. Next: the golden trick of overfitting check — overfitting a single batch!
